{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:転送]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

{{Infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | Image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | Location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:redirect]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

{{Infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

{{Infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

**30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

{{Infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

**30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]]

**30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:Takijiri Ōji]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}]], [[Tanabe City|{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}]], Nakahechi Town, Kurisugawa 859 | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子}}''' is a **shrine** located in **{{ill|Tanabe City|ja|田辺市}}, Wakayama Prefecture, Japan**. The **official name** under religious law today is **"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"** (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the **{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}**, counted among the **{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子}}** (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the **National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"** (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - **{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}** - **{{ill|Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命}}** - **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}}** - **{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命}}** - **{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命}}** However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at **{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}**), during its time as one of the **Kujūku Ōji**, the shrine was associated with **{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩}}** as its **honji suijaku** (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as **1081** (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by **1109** (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as **the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano**, as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the **medieval period**, Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in **Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"** (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and **Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record** (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the **confluence of the Iwata River** (now **{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)}}**) and the **Ishibune River**, where the rapid currents create a **waterfall-like sound**Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at **Takijiri Ōji**, pilgrims would **offer sacred cloth (hōbei)** and **purify themselves by immersing in the river**. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the **Jōkyū War** (*1221*), the decline of **imperial pilgrimages** led to **neglect**. In **1229**, Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the **Muromachi period**, the difficulty of crossing the **Iwata River** led pilgrims to use **Shiomi Pass**, bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the **Edo period**, Takijiri Ōji was known as **"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"**. In **1868**, it was renamed **"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"**, and in **1908**, it was merged with **11 local shrines**, becoming **Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)**Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - **Takijiri Ōji Ruins** (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - **Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")** (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - **30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s** {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|ja|紀伊田辺駅}} == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{DEFAULTSORT:Takijiri Oji}} [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabe City Landmarks]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takijiri-ōji]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takijiri Ōji-qq]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:Takijiri Ōji]]
