The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Kujūku Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:和歌山県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]]

Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の重要文化財]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}}''' is a '''shrine''' located in '''[[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe City]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan'''. The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri-ōji]] [[en:Takijiri Ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]]

The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:田辺市の歴史]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kujuku-ojis]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}}''' is a '''shrine''' located in '''[[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe City]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan'''. The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - '''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}}''' is a '''shrine''' located in '''[[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe City]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan'''. The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - '''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:和歌山県内のMunicipal designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - '''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[ja:%E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[sn:E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}} | image = [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | ISO = JP-30 | LatD = 33 | LatM = 46 | LatS = 31.07 | LonD =135 | LonM = 30 | LonS = 12.63 | Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]
{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri (流造) | Festival = [[November 3]] | Map = Japan Wakayama }} '''{{ill|Takijiri Ōji|ja|滝尻王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takijiri Ōji|qq=}}''' is a '''shrine''' located in '''[[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe City]], Wakayama Prefecture, Japan'''. The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - '''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[sn:E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

The '''official name''' under religious law today is '''"Takijiri Ōji Miya Jukkō Shrine"''' (滝尻王子宮十郷神社){{Cite web|ja|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7030|title=Takijiri Ōji Jukkō Shrine|work=[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/ Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association] (Official Website)|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. It was originally one of the '''{{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}''', counted among the '''{{ill|Gotai Ōji|ja|五体王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotai Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Five Great Ōji Shrines) as mentioned in *"Kumano Gongen Zao Hōden Zokō Nikki"*. It is part of the '''National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes"''' (*designated on November 2, 2000*){{Cite web|ja |url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html |title=Tanabe City Designated Cultural Properties – Monuments |publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education |accessdate=2009-03-20 }}. == Enshrined Deities == Currently, the shrine enshrines five deities: - '''{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}''' - '''{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|日子火能迩々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' - '''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]]''' - '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|日子穂々手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' - '''{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜茅葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' However, according to the *Kumano Engi* (*1326*, stored at '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''), during its time as one of the '''Ninety-Nine Ōji''', the shrine was associated with '''{{ill|Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|ja|不空羂索菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukū Kenjaku Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' as its '''honji suijaku''' (*Buddhist counterpart of the deity*)Heibonsha [1997: 527]. == Overview == [[File:Sacred arch of TAKIJIRI-oji shrine.JPG|thumb|250px|Torii gate]] [[File:Takijiri-oji shrine 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of the worship hall from the approach]] === Entrance to Kumano Sanzan === The name *Takijiri* appears as early as '''1081''' (*Eihō 1*) in *Tamefusa Kyōki*, and by '''1109''' (*Tennin 2*), the *Chūyūki* mentions the existence of the Ōji shrine. It was regarded as '''the entrance to the sacred lands of Kumano''', as stated in *Chūyūki*: *"For the first time, entering the mountains of the gods"*. A similar description appears in *Genpei Seisuiki*Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74, 76]Heibonsha [1997: 527-528]. By the '''medieval period''', Takijiri Ōji had lodgings for pilgrims, as hinted in '''Fujiwara Teika’s "Kumano Michi no Aida Gugoki"''' (*Kumano Route Notes*, from *Meigetsuki*, 1201) and '''Fujiwara Tsunemitsu’s pilgrimage record''' (*Minkei-ki*, 1229)Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 76]. The name *Takijiri* comes from its location at the '''confluence of the Iwata River''' (now '''{{ill|Tonda River (Wakayama)|ja|富田川 (和歌山県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonda River (Wakayama)|qq=}}''') and the '''Ishibune River''', where the rapid currents create a '''waterfall-like sound'''Kumanoji Editorial Committee [1973: 74]. Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[sn:E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]

Upon arriving at '''Takijiri Ōji''', pilgrims would '''offer sacred cloth (hōbei)''' and '''purify themselves by immersing in the river'''. The *Kumano Engi* describes the purification ritual as follows: {{Quotation|The river to the right is water that invokes Kannon, and the river to the left is medicinal water that removes illness.|*Kumano Engi*Heibonsha [1997: 528]}} It further states: {{Quotation|Above the bridge, the Iwata River flows from the Pure Land of Senju Kannon, while the Ishibune River comes from Yakushi Nyorai’s Lapis Lazuli Pure Land. Bathing in these waters washes away sins from infinite past lives.|*Kumano Engi*}} === Decline and Transformation === After the '''Jōkyū War''' (*1221*), the decline of '''imperial pilgrimages''' led to '''neglect'''. In '''1229''', Fujiwara Tsunemitsu wrote: {{Quotation|All along the route, the Ōji shrines are destroyed and collapsed, a truly sorrowful sight... Takijiri Ōji remains standing, yet it too is in ruins.|*Minkei-ki* (1229)}} By the '''Muromachi period''', the difficulty of crossing the '''Iwata River''' led pilgrims to use '''Shiomi Pass''', bypassing Takijiri Ōji entirely. By the '''Edo period''', Takijiri Ōji was known as '''"Takijiri Gotai Ōji Shrine"'''. In '''1868''', it was renamed '''"Takijiri Ōji Shrine"''', and in '''1908''', it was merged with '''11 local shrines''', becoming '''Jukkō Shrine (十郷神社)'''Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association [1995]. == Cultural Properties == - '''Takijiri Ōji Ruins''' (*Part of the National Historic Site "Kumano Pilgrimage Routes", designated on November 2, 2000*) - '''Black Lacquered Short Sword (signed "Aritsugu")''' (*National Important Cultural Property*, designated May 30, 1972){{National Cultural Property Database|6680}} == Access == - '''30 minutes by bus from JR Kisei Main Line’s''' [[:en:Kii-Tanabe Station|Kii-Tanabe Station]] == External Links == - [http://www.aikis.or.jp/~nakahech/sightseeing/kodoukan/ Kumano Kōdōkan] - Official website {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|bottom}} {{translated page|ja|滝尻王子|version=98185624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Takijiri Ōji]] [[fr:Takijiri-ōji]] [[sn:E6%BB%9D%E5%B0%BB%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[sn:akijiri Ōji]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Tanabe, Wakayama]] [[ja:滝尻王子]]
